summary: Large mnName name-menu decompilation slice. The PR implemented many previously stubbed functions for name-list display, sorting, deletion, scrolling, input handling, confirmation UI, archive loading, and text rendering, while tightening headers and GALE01 data symbols. The decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 matched code at 60.49% (+0.10%, +3956 bytes), with 11 new matches in main/melee/mn/mnname and several larger remaining partials improved substantially.

key_files.role: Main implementation: added 1400+ lines replacing many mnName stubs with name-list logic, UI animation/text rendering, deletion, sorting, input handling, archive loading, and matching helpers.
Header cleanup: added MnName_GObj.text2 and converted many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or incorrect integer name arguments into concrete prototypes.

Data-symbol refinement: split mnName_803ED618 from one 0x440 object into a 0x18 object plus separate string/data symbols mnName_803ED630, mnName_803ED644, mnName_803ED650, mnName_803ED668, and mnName_803ED672.
Prototype tightening: gmMainLib_8015DBF4 changed from UNK_PARAMS to a concrete s32 parameter, matching its use when deleting a name index.

what_changed: Implemented or heavily advanced mnName functions including CompareNameStrings, DeleteName, mnName_SortNames, mnName_80237D94, mnName_ConfirmNameDeleteInput, mnName_MainInput, mnName_80238754, mnName_80238AE0, mnName_80238C34, fn_80239574, mnName_80239878, mnName_80239A24, fn_8023A0BC, mnName_8023A290, mnName_8023A59C, mnName_8023A9B4, and mnName_8023AC40.
Added static mnName_NameDisplayOrder[0x78] for display/sort order and used it when rendering or deleting visible name entries.
Added MnNameArchive with joint, anim_joint, matanim_joint, and shapeanim_joint fields for archive-backed UI assets.
Changed IsNameUnique and IsNameNotAllowed to take char* name data instead of integer slot/name_idx arguments, aligning them with CompareNameStrings(char*, char*).
Added GetNumNameList and GetNameCount_noinline helpers and reused name-count logic for IsNameListFull, mnName_GetColumnCount, scrolling, and menu state.
Implemented name comparison behavior around mnName_StringTerminator and checkStringRest, including handling of apparent padding/rest characters via mnName_804D4BF0.
Implemented name deletion by clearing/initializing the selected name, compacting persistent name data, re-sorting display order, refreshing the visible list, and calling gmMainLib_8015DBF4(nameIdx).
Implemented main name-list input flow for A/B, d-pad, L/R page/column scrolling, add-name, sort toggle, delete-confirm entry, and error/confirm/cursor audio feedback via lbAudioAx_80024030.
Added UI construction and refresh paths using GObj_Create, HSD_JObjLoadJoint, HSD_JObjAddAnimAll, HSD_SisLib text objects, scrollbar visibility/position updates, and language-specific text swaps.
Loaded many archive sections from mn_804D6BB8 through lbArchive_LoadSections, including language-dependent AutoNamesList and NotAllowedNamesList.
smart_moves: The display-order buffer keeps sorted presentation separate from persistent name storage; deletion compacts actual persistent data while sorting can simply permute indices.
The symbol split in GALE01 avoided treating mixed vector/string/data content as one giant 0x440 blob, making later data matching and naming more precise.
Concrete char* signatures for name comparison, uniqueness, and disallowed-name checks make call sites describe the actual data flow instead of hiding it behind slot integers.
The PR reused shared count logic through GetNumNameList/GetNameCount_noinline rather than duplicating 0x78-slot scans everywhere.
Small noinline wrapper helpers around GetNameCount, mnName_80238754, mnName_802388D4, and mnName_80238964 gave call-site-level control useful for matching UI codegen.
The implementation preserved menu constants visible in the assembly model: 0x78 name slots, 6 entries per column, 0x18 visible name buttons, and special selections 0x18/0x19/0x1A.
The added MnName_GObj.text2 field captures a discovered UI text object instead of leaving it as unstructured user-data storage.
decomp_lessons: For menu decompilation, track both persistent data and presentation order; the game may sort by an index array rather than moving records until deletion/compaction.
Name strings in this subsystem are not plain strcmp inputs: matching code must account for mnName_StringTerminator and the repeated rest/padding pattern checked by checkStringRest.
GObj fields such as gx_link, p_priority, and p_link can carry menu-specific state like scroll column, sort mode, and animation state; validate them from call sites before inventing new struct fields.
Header prototypes should be tightened as soon as call sites prove types; this PR changed several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations to concrete HSD_GObj*, u8, s32, void, and char* signatures.
Data symbols should be split as soon as the diff reveals typed regions such as Vec3 arrays and string literals; doing so helps .data/.sdata matching and future naming.

For UI code, resource lifecycle details matter for matching and correctness: this PR repeatedly frees/replaces HSD_SisLib text with HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4 and removes/rebuilds JObj children when refreshing the list.
Language branches are part of the functional model: lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS selects name text arrays and archive sections for AutoNamesList/NotAllowedNamesList.
naming_conventions: Address-style names such as mnName_80237D94 and fn_80239574 were retained for unresolved functions, while clearer existing names like GetNameText, GetNameCount, IsNameListFull, CompareNameStrings, IsNameUnique, DeleteName, and IsNameNotAllowed were used where semantics were known.
The static display-order buffer was named mnName_NameDisplayOrder, matching the mnName_ prefix and describing its role without inventing a broader type.
Temporary matching shims use a _noinline suffix, for example GetNameCount_noinline, mnName_80238754_noinline, mnName_802388D4_noinline, and mnName_80238964_noinline.
Archive asset grouping was named MnNameArchive with direct field names joint, anim_joint, matanim_joint, and shapeanim_joint.
Unknown globals kept address-based mnName_803/804 names until evidence justified better names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK annotations, including PAD_STACK(8) and PAD_STACK(0x20), in several UI/input functions to shape stack layout.
Introduced noinline wrappers and routed selected call sites through them, an explicit call-boundary tactic visible in mnName_80238A04, mnName_80239878, mnName_8023A59C, and related helpers.
Kept assembly-shaped locals, casts, and comma expressions such as (0, mnName_803ED538) and repeated u8/s8 casts where needed for codegen-sensitive matching.
Reworked mnName_8023749C with a u8 slot cast stored in an unsigned short temporary and a local terminator comparison, moving it from 97.84% to 100% according to the bot.
Centralized the name-count loop but preserved call/noinline behavior where the bot showed small byte-level matches for IsNameListFull and mnName_GetColumnCount.
Split the data symbol map around mnName_803ED618 into typed vector/string/data pieces, which is a useful data-matching tactic when one object actually contains multiple logical assets.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported 11 new 100% matches: fn_80239574, mnName_80237D94, fn_8023A0BC, mnName_80239878, DeleteName, mnName_80238754, mnName_80238AE0, mnName_8023749C, IsNameListFull, mnName_GetColumnCount, and mnName_80238A04.

Reported partial improvements including mnName_80238C34 at 89.27%, mnName_80239A24 at 89.55%, mnName_8023A59C at 99.30%, mnName_8023AC40 at 84.20%, mnName_MainInput at 91.90%, mnName_SortNames at 89.02%, and CompareNameStrings at 95.71%.
No PR body, human reviews, or inline human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching mnName_80238C34, mnName_80239A24, mnName_8023A59C, mnName_8023AC40, mnName_MainInput, mnName_SortNames, or CompareNameStrings to see how the remaining partial matches were completed.
Search for mnName_804D4BF0 and checkStringRest to determine the semantic name for the name-string padding/rest character pattern.
Search for gmMainLib_8015DBF4(s32) call sites to name the game-mode routine that is notified after a name slot is deleted.
Search for MnName_GObj.text2 and 0x44 user-data allocations to validate or improve the MnName_GObj struct layout.
Search later symbol-map changes around mnName_803ED618 through mnName_803ED672 for final data/string names.
